---
title: "AI in Research: Harnessing the Strengths, Controlling the Weaknesses"
subtitle: "Tools and Techniques for Responsible AI Use in Research"
author: "Guido Biele"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    css: styles/KI4Forsking-reveal.css
    slide-number: true
    transition: slide
    embed-resources: true
---

## 

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:30%; font-size:0.5em;"}
```
Download hsbc data here: https://data-browser.hbsc.org/wp-content/uploads/csvs/data.csv  and meta data here https://data-browser.hbsc.org/wp-content/uploads/csvs/metadata.csv.
Then extract data for 15 year olds 
- who-5-well-being-index 
- life satisfaction
- self efficacy (2 measures)
from the data and calculate a composite score for each country.

Next go to https://zenodo.org/records/13382904 and download the PISA 2022 student file.
Build a composite score from reading, mathematics and science, and compute the average for each European country.

Finally, plot a scatterplot with the composite HBSC score on the x-axis and the composite PISA score on the y-axis. Label all the Nordic countries and highlight Norway and save the figure as figures/scatter_pisa_hsbc.png

Use R to do this.
Document all steps in a script, which you save as scripts/pisa_hbsc_scatter.
Save all data in the data folder.
```
:::

::: {.column width="70%"}
![](figures/pisa_hbsc_composite_scatter.png){width=100% fig-align="center"}
:::
::::


## LLM problems for researchers

* LLMs are **prediction / interpolations machines**: They model the temporal order of tokens (words)
* This is **very useful**, because words that are correctly ordered do contain meaning
* But the way LLMS are built produces 2 problems for researchers when "new" chains of words are generated
  * **Regression to the mode** of the training data
  * **Hallucination**

As researchers, we want to harness the usefulness of LLMs while controlling their problems

---

## Solutions for LLM problems

* Enable **extended reasoning** for complex questions^[Called "thinking mode", "deep research", etc. depending on provider]
* Good **custom instructions**
* Good **prompts**
* Fill the **context windows** with relevant information
* Use tools with **inbuilt verification**
* **Only ask LLMs to do work you can verify**^[LLMs multiply the competences you have, they don't give you completely new ones.]
* **Data privacy**: see the [blog post](AI4Research_blog_en.html)

---

## Extended reasoning

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
* Most chatbots offer an **extended reasoning mode**^[Labels vary — *thinking*, *deep think*, *extended thinking*, *deep research*, depending on provider.]
* With it on, the model works **step by step before** answering 
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
<video autoplay loop muted playsinline width="100%" style="display: block; margin: 0 auto;" title="Extended reasoning in Copilot">
  <source src="figures/Thinking.mp4" type="video/mp4" />
</video>
:::

::::

* **Auto** lets the model pick depth; for **demanding analytic work**, **choose a higher setting explicitly**

---

## Custom instructions

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="60%"}
* All models allow to **change the personality**
* Apply to **all conversations** — set once, always in effect
* Instructions for researchers
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
<video autoplay loop muted playsinline width="100%" style="display: block; margin: 0 auto;" title="Step-by-step: opening Copilot settings, navigating to Personalization, and pasting custom instructions into the text field" aria-label="Screen recording showing how to set custom instructions in Microsoft Copilot 365: open Settings via the profile icon, select Personalization, click Revise instructions, paste the instructions, and save.">
  <source src="figures/CustomInstructions.mp4" type="video/mp4" />
</video>
:::

::::

> - **Say "I don't know"** rather than guessing; never fabricate citations or statistics
> - **Use tools** like web search or python to verify facts before stating them
> - **Push back** on flawed reasoning or unsupported claims
> - **Think step by step** and check your reasoning before answering
> - **Cite primary literature**; prefer peer-reviewed sources over summaries
> - **Read papers critically**: flag when conclusions outrun the evidence, when study designs cannot support causal claims, or when limitations are understated
> - **Be concise**; do not pad responses or ask unnecessary follow-up questions
> - Do not suggest next steps or offer further help unless asked

## Custom chatbots

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
* Define and use task-specific chatbots
* Provide additional information about desired behavior
* Examples:
  * Methods consultant, copy editor English, grant reviewer, copy editor plain language
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
<video autoplay loop muted playsinline width="100%" style="display: block; margin: 0 auto;" title="Step-by-step: creating a custom agent in Microsoft Copilot 365 with a name, instructions, and uploaded knowledge documents" aria-label="Screen recording showing how to create a custom agent in Microsoft Copilot 365: clicking New agent in the sidebar, entering a name and description, pasting instructions, uploading knowledge documents, and clicking Create.">
  <source src="figures/CustomChatbot.mp4" type="video/mp4" />
</video>
:::

::::



Chatbot contents

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="30%"}
* Name
* Description
:::

::: {.column width="30%"}
* **Instructions**
* **Knowledge**
:::

::: {.column width="30%"}
* Tools
:::

::::


## Retrieval Augmented Generation

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
* Limits answers to those derived from provided text
* Uses semantic search to find relevant text in a knowledge base
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
<video autoplay loop muted playsinline width="100%" style="display: block; margin: 0 auto;" title="NotebookLM interface: uploading sources, asking a question, and clicking cited passages to verify the source text" aria-label="Screen recording showing the NotebookLM interface: uploading PDF sources, typing a research question, receiving an answer with inline citations, and clicking a citation to see the exact quoted passage in the original document.">
  <source src="figures/NotebookLM.mp4" type="video/mp4" />
</video>
:::
::::

  * **Elicit** (elicit.com, great but uses mostly abstracts :-()
  * **NotebookLM** (https://notebooklm.google.com/)
* Also here: Garbage in, garbage out. (`pdfParser` in R)




## AI-assisted text generation — with verification

* FHI produces reports for hundreds of municipalities, schools, ... — writing subgroup-specific text by hand doesn't scale
* LLMs can generate this text automatically from statistical tables
* Also useful for drafting results sections from analysis output
* Risk: LLMs can hallucinate numbers —> **programmatic verification** ^[In R: `tblscribe` compares numbers in generated text against source table]

## Agentic systems

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="80%"}
  * Plan and execute multi-step tasks with minimal human input.
  * Can use external tools: web search, file system, databases and APIs.
  * **Write and execute code.**
  * Work iteratively — run code, read errors, and **self-correct**.
  * Most effective when you can judge whether the output is correct.

:::

::: {.column style="width:20%; font-size:0.5em;"}
  * cursor.com
  * anthropic.com/product/claude-code
  * anthropic.com/product/claude-cowork
  * https://antigravity.google/
  * ...

:::

::::

## Summary

* LLMs are extremely powerful, but also fallible
* Need guardrails: Custom instructions, Custom Chatbots, RAG, Programmatic verification
* Agentic systems are the next step
* LLMs multiply the competences you already have

**We are living the future now: The question is not if LLMs will change how we do research, but how.**
